company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Senior secured single-draw term loan under Loan Agreement dated as of May 8, 2024 (the 'Closing Date'); Ankura Trust Company, LLC as Collateral Agent and administrative agent.
plain_english_summary: The controlling secured debt is a single term loan closed May 8, 2024. Ankura is the agent; lenders are Barings entities.
notes: Company renamed to Coherus Oncology, Inc. effective 2025-05-29 (FY2025 10-K).

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Sole Borrower and a Credit Party is Coherus BioSciences, Inc., a Delaware corporation.
plain_english_summary: Coherus itself is the borrower.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Ankura Trust Company, LLC (New Hampshire) serves as both administrative agent and Collateral Agent ('as the administrative agent and collateral agent').
plain_english_summary: One agent, Ankura, plays both roles for the lender group.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Lender names redacted [***] in Exhibit D of the public filing; agreement references a 'Barings Fee Letter'. Confirmed via Barings BDC and sister funds' Schedules of Investments that Barings entities hold the Coherus term loan.
plain_english_summary: The lenders are Barings-managed funds (including Barings BDC / BBDC). Names are blacked out in the public loan agreement but confirmed through Barings' own SEC filings.
notes: Lender identity confirmed externally; syndicate composition and BBDC allocation not fully public.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Aggregate Commitment redacted [***] in Exhibit D; resolved from FY2025 10-K: '2029 Term Loan' with $38.7M principal, ~$37.5M net proceeds after 3.0% original issue discount. Stated principal carried at $38,660K.
plain_english_summary: The loan is ~$38.7 million (about $37.5M in cash after the discount). This is a small facility, NOT the ~$250M predecessor loan.
notes: Redacted in loan agreement; figure taken from FY2025 10-K debt note. Predecessor 2027 Term Loan ($250M, BioPharma/Pharmakon) was repaid at this closing.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Single-draw term loan funded in full on the Closing Date; 'may not be re-borrowed'. No delayed-draw, accordion or incremental facility.
plain_english_summary: One-time draw, fully funded at close. No ability to borrow more.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Fully drawn at close; no revolver or committed undrawn amount.
plain_english_summary: No undrawn availability.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: 'solely to repay in full all Indebtedness ... under the Existing Credit Agreement ... and to fund its general corporate and working capital requirements'; no Margin Stock / purpose credit.
plain_english_summary: Proceeds refinanced the old BioPharma/Pharmakon term loan and fund general corporate needs.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: 'Term Loan Maturity Date' = the 5th anniversary of the Closing Date, i.e., May 8, 2029. Term Loan due and payable in full on that date.
plain_english_summary: The loan matures May 8, 2029 (5-year term).
notes: Confirmed in FY2025 10-K and Q1 2026 10-Q.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Scheduled amortization is '[reserved]'; principal 'is due and payable in full on the Term Loan Maturity Date'. Interest-only with bullet at maturity.
plain_english_summary: No principal amortization; interest-only until a single balloon payment at maturity.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Entire principal (currently $38,660K) plus accrued interest due at maturity; no prepayment premium due at scheduled maturity.
plain_english_summary: The full ~$38.7M principal comes due as a lump sum on May 8, 2029.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Term Loan Rate = 'Term SOFR for the Interest Period therefor plus the Applicable Margin'; Term SOFR = 3-month CME Term SOFR set 2 business days before each Interest Period.
plain_english_summary: Floating rate based on 3-month SOFR, reset quarterly.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: 'Applicable Margin' = 'a rate per annum equal to eight percent (8.00%)'. All-in coupon = 3-month Term SOFR + 8.00% (~12% all-in at recent SOFR).
plain_english_summary: Margin is a fixed 8.00% over SOFR, i.e., roughly 12% all-in currently.
notes: 10-K discloses coupon as '8.0% plus a three-month SOFR rate'; ~12.0% all-in.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: 'Floor' = 1.00% per annum, defined and applied within the Benchmark Replacement mechanics; the base Term Loan Rate definition does not restate a floor. FY2025 10-K does not restate a floor for the 2029 loan.
plain_english_summary: A 1.00% SOFR floor is defined but non-binding today since SOFR is well above 1%.
notes: Ambiguity flagged: floor appears in benchmark-replacement language, not the primary coupon. Non-binding at current rates.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Interest payable quarterly in arrears; entirely cash-pay. No PIK option in Section 2.
plain_english_summary: All interest is paid in cash quarterly; nothing is capitalized.
notes: Cash-pay (vs. PIK) is lender-favorable for a pre-profit biotech.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No payment-in-kind interest provision.
plain_english_summary: No PIK toggle; interest cannot be deferred into principal.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Default Rate = 'four percentage points (4.00%) above the rate that is otherwise applicable', payable in cash on demand.
plain_english_summary: On default, interest jumps 4 points, payable in cash on demand.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No undrawn/commitment fee (single-draw, fully funded).
plain_english_summary: No unused-line fee.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: 'Additional Consideration' = Commitment x 0.03 (3.00%), fully earned, non-refundable, 'treated as original issue discount', deducted from loan proceeds at funding.
plain_english_summary: A 3% original issue discount is netted from funding (~$1.2M), so Coherus received ~$37.5M on a ~$38.7M loan.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Agent and lender fees payable per separate Barings Fee Letter and Agent/Agency Fee Letter; amounts not in the public filing.
plain_english_summary: Additional agent/lender fees exist in side letters but the dollar amounts are not public.
notes: Not calculable from public information.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No 'Final Payment' or exit-fee term defined in the Loan Agreement; any such economics would reside in Fee Letters (not public).
plain_english_summary: No exit fee is stated in the agreement itself.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Prepayment Premium = principal prepaid x 10% (on/after 1st before 2nd anniversary), 5% (2nd-3rd), 0% (after 3rd). None due on principal paid at scheduled maturity.
plain_english_summary: Early payoff costs 10% in year 2, 5% in year 3, nothing after year 3 or at maturity.
notes: Confirmed in FY2025 10-K.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Makewhole Amount (prepayment/acceleration before 1st anniversary) = 'all interest that would have accrued and been payable ... through the 1st anniversary' on the prepaid principal; due on acceleration as liquidated damages.
plain_english_summary: Paying off in the first year requires making the lenders whole for a full year of interest; the 1st anniversary (May 2025) has now passed.
notes: Make-whole window expired 2025-05-08; only the step-down premium now applies.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
threshold_or_amount: Last day of each calendar quarter
legal_summary: 'Interest Date' = last day of each calendar quarter; interest paid quarterly in arrears; Interest Period is 3 months from the Closing Date.
plain_english_summary: Interest is due at the end of each calendar quarter.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Interest 'computed on the basis of a year of 360 days and the actual number of days elapsed'.
plain_english_summary: Interest accrues on an actual/360 basis (slightly higher effective yield).

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: 3.00% OID via 'Additional Consideration', deducted at funding and treated as OID; ~$1.6M unamortized discount at 12/31/2025.
plain_english_summary: The 3% discount accretes into interest expense over the loan's life.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Stated principal $38,660K at 3/31/2026 (net carrying value $37,147K); flat since inception, interest-only, no paydowns.
plain_english_summary: As of Q1 2026 the loan is still ~$38.7M outstanding, unchanged.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Effective all-in rate ~12.0% (Q4 2025); FY2025 interest expense $5.1M on the 2029 Term Loan.
plain_english_summary: The loan currently costs roughly 12% per year.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: First-priority security interest in 'all of such Credit Party's ... properties and assets constituting Collateral, whether now existing or hereafter acquired' (subject to Permitted Liens); enumerated grant in the redacted Guaranty and Security Agreement (Exhibit C).
plain_english_summary: The lenders hold a blanket first-priority lien on substantially all assets of the borrower and guarantors.
notes: Security Agreement (Exhibit C) fully redacted [***]; exact enumerated Collateral/Excluded Property list not public.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: IP is collateral, perfected via a separate Intellectual Property Security Agreement (a 'Collateral Document'); covers patents, trademarks, copyrights, trade secrets, software, domain names; patent assignments recorded with USPTO. Covenant to maintain rights to commercialize UDENYCA and LOQTORZI.
plain_english_summary: The lenders' lien reaches Coherus's IP (patents/trademarks), recorded at the USPTO.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Pledge of all Equity Interests (other than Excluded Equity Interests) in each Subsidiary (other than Excluded Subsidiaries); foreign subs capped at 65% voting / 100% non-voting only where a full pledge triggers a Sec. 956 adverse tax consequence.
plain_english_summary: Subsidiary stock is pledged; foreign subs get the customary 65% voting cap only for tax reasons.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Control Agreement required for each Collateral Account within 30 days of opening (45 days post-closing for existing accounts, per Sec. 5.5; Sec. 5.14(b) states 30 days - internal inconsistency flagged); non-terminable without agent consent.
plain_english_summary: Bank accounts must be under lender control agreements, giving lenders the ability to sweep cash on default.
notes: Conflicting cure windows (30 vs 45 days) between Sec.5.5 and Sec.5.14(b) - flagged, not resolved silently.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Excluded Accounts = payroll/benefits, zero-balance (swept daily), escrow/fiduciary, merchant, legally-restricted, third-party cash-collateral accounts, plus any officer-designated accounts whose aggregate balance never exceeds $750,000.
plain_english_summary: Small/operational accounts are carved out of control; catch-all designated accounts capped at $750K total.
notes: Relevant to minimum-liquidity: only unrestricted cash in Collateral Accounts counts toward the covenant.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: 'Excluded Property' has the meaning in the Security Agreement (Exhibit C), which is redacted; full enumerated list not public. Related carve-outs (Excluded Accounts, Excluded Equity Interests, Excluded Subsidiaries) are in the Loan Agreement.
plain_english_summary: There is an excluded-property list, but the details are blacked out in the public filing.
notes: Not fully calculable from public information.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
applicable_entity: Coherus Intermediate Corp., Intekrin Therapeutics Inc., Surface Oncology, LLC, and Coherus Oncology Supportive Care LLC
legal_summary: Guaranty via the Guaranty and Security Agreement by the four named Credit Party guarantors; the covenant (Sec. 5.12) also requires each future Subsidiary other than Excluded Subsidiaries to guarantee and grant collateral, and new subs to join within 45 days (Sec. 5.13).
plain_english_summary: The four named guarantors (Coherus Intermediate Corp., Intekrin Therapeutics Inc., Surface Oncology, LLC, and Coherus Oncology Supportive Care LLC) guarantee the loan and pledge assets; the covenant sweeps future subsidiaries in within 45 days.
notes: Surface Oncology is the sole significant subsidiary per FY2025 10-K Ex-21.1.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
applicable_entity: Coherus Immunology LLC; subs with assets < $2M
legal_summary: Excluded Subsidiaries = subs where lien/pledge legally prohibited or requires unobtained consent; non-wholly-owned subs where prohibited; subs with aggregate asset FMV < $2,000,000; Coherus Immunology LLC (named, no assets at closing); or mutually-agreed excessive-cost subs.
plain_english_summary: Immaterial subsidiaries (under $2M assets) and legally-restricted subs need not guarantee.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Fraudulent-conveyance savings clause resides in the redacted Guaranty and Security Agreement; not extractable from the public filing.
plain_english_summary: A standard fraudulent-transfer cap on guarantees is expected but the text is not public.
notes: Not calculable from public information.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Perfection by UCC filings, deposit-account control agreements, IP Security Agreement (USPTO recordation), certificated-equity delivery; post-closing items on Schedule 5.14 within 30 days; real property > $1M mortgaged within 60 days.
plain_english_summary: Lenders perfect through UCC filings, account control, IP recordation and stock delivery, with short post-closing deadlines.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Liens/guarantees released on payment in full, on permitted dispositions (other than to a Credit Party), with Required-Lender consent, or when a Person ceases to be a Subsidiary via a permitted transaction; agent may subordinate to specified Permitted Liens.
plain_english_summary: Collateral is released when the loan is repaid or assets are sold in permitted deals - normal mechanics.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Facility is secured by a first-priority perfected lien; Intercreditor Agreement dated the Closing Date names the Collateral Agent as 'senior agent' and Coduet Royalty Holdings, LLC as purchaser.
plain_english_summary: The term lenders are first in line (senior); the royalty buyer (Coduet) is junior to them.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
applicable_entity: Collateral Agent / Coduet Royalty Holdings, LLC
legal_summary: Intercreditor Agreement between Ankura (senior agent) and Coduet (royalty purchaser) governs priority; RPRPSA states the royalty buyer's lien and remedies are 'subject to the provisions of the Intercreditor Agreement', which 'shall govern and control'.
plain_english_summary: A signed intercreditor agreement subordinates the Coduet royalty claim to the term loan.
notes: Intercreditor Agreement itself not in the public exhibit set; terms summarized from cross-references.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Shall not 'permit Liquidity to be less than $15,000,000' from and after the Closing Date. 'Liquidity' = unrestricted cash and Cash Equivalents of the Credit Parties and Subsidiaries held in any Collateral Accounts. Breach is an Event of Default under Sec. 7.2(a)(ii) with no identified cure period.
plain_english_summary: Coherus must keep at least $15M of unrestricted cash in controlled Collateral Accounts at all times; falling below is a covenant breach with no identified cure period. This is the ONLY financial maintenance covenant.
notes: Covenant counts only unrestricted cash in Collateral Accounts - a narrower measure than total reported cash. In compliance through 3/31/2026.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No leverage ratio, interest/fixed-charge coverage, minimum revenue, minimum EBITDA, or other financial ratio covenant. Minimum Liquidity is the sole financial maintenance covenant. (A positive-EBITDA / 105%-of-loan liquidity test exists only as a condition to Permitted Acquisitions, not as an ongoing covenant.)
plain_english_summary: There are no leverage, coverage, or revenue covenants - unusually light for a term loan, reflecting the small size and cash-rich borrower.
notes: Documentary weakness: a single liquidity covenant provides limited early warning of credit deterioration.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Company reports full compliance at 9/30/2025, 12/31/2025 and 3/31/2026; no breach or waiver disclosed. Total cash/equivalents/marketable securities $167.0M and cash alone $115.2M at 3/31/2026 vs. the $15M minimum.
plain_english_summary: Reported cash sits well above the $15M floor, but the covenant counts only unrestricted cash in controlled Collateral Accounts, so the apparent multiple-times headroom is indicative rather than a precise covenant calculation. Coherus reports it is in compliance.
notes: Not all reported cash/marketable securities necessarily qualify as unrestricted cash in Collateral Accounts; headroom is indicative.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Audited annual financials within 90 days of fiscal year-end (or earlier 10-K filing); report 'shall not be subject to any qualifications ... other than a going concern qualification under ASC 205-40'. EDGAR filing = deemed delivery.
plain_english_summary: Audited annuals due within 90 days; a going-concern qualification is expressly permitted (a borrower-friendly carve-out).
notes: Permitting a going-concern qualification weakens the audit-quality protection.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Unaudited quarterly financials within 45 days of quarter-end (or earlier 10-Q filing); EDGAR filing = deemed delivery.
plain_english_summary: Quarterly financials due within 45 days (satisfied by the 10-Q).

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No monthly financial reporting required.
plain_english_summary: No monthly reporting obligation.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Compliance Certificate with (or within 5 business days of deemed delivery of) each quarterly/annual financial delivery; signed by a Responsible Officer certifying fair presentation and no Default.
plain_english_summary: A signed compliance certificate accompanies each set of financials.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No annual budget, board-approved operating plan, or projections delivery covenant under Sec. 5.2.
plain_english_summary: No budget or forecast delivery requirement - a reporting gap.
notes: Absence of budget/projection reporting reduces forward visibility for lenders.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Written notice within 5 business days after a Responsible Officer's knowledge of any Default/Event of Default, ERISA Event, or Material Adverse Change.
plain_english_summary: Coherus must flag defaults, ERISA events, and material adverse changes within 5 business days.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Prompt notice (deemed given via timely 8-K) of investigations/litigation exceeding Exchange Act materiality thresholds, or alleging Health Care/FDA/data-protection violations re a Specified Product that could reasonably result in a Material Adverse Change.
plain_english_summary: Material lawsuits and FDA/regulatory actions touching the products must be reported promptly.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Notify Collateral Agent of material IP challenges/proceedings; written notice within 30 days of entering any Restricted License.
plain_english_summary: Coherus must report material IP disputes and new restricted licenses.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Collateral Agent has access to Collateral, books and records and may conduct audits/field exams (Sec. 5.9); IP and account information rights elsewhere.
plain_english_summary: Lenders may inspect collateral and books and conduct audits.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No Transfer of Collateral or Specified-Product Company IP except Permitted Transfers (ordinary-course inventory, obsolete equipment, intercompany between Credit Parties, Permitted CH-131 license/spin-off, general basket, and redacted clauses (r),(s),(t)).
plain_english_summary: Asset sales are restricted to defined permitted categories; some monetization baskets are blacked out.
notes: Permitted Transfers clauses (r),(s),(t) redacted [***] - these interact with junior-debt redemption.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Only Permitted Indebtedness allowed (see baskets). Includes convertible-note capacity, capital leases, acquisition financing, contingent obligations, and general unsecured debt.
plain_english_summary: New debt is limited to enumerated permitted baskets.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Only Permitted Liens (existing, tax, statutory/landlord, capex-securing, acquisition/assumed, earnest-money). Negative pledge in Sec. 6.6 with limited Permitted Negative Pledge exceptions.
plain_english_summary: Assets can't be pledged elsewhere except for narrow permitted liens; a negative-pledge protects the collateral.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No mergers/acquisitions/investments except Permitted Acquisitions and Permitted Investments; Permitted Acquisition requires no Default, related business, collateral joinder, and either target positive trailing-4-qtr EBITDA or pro forma Liquidity >= 105% of outstanding Term Loans for 12 months plus board approval.
plain_english_summary: M&A and investments are gated by baskets and, for acquisitions, a profitability or 105%-liquidity test.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No dividends/redemptions except Permitted Distributions (e.g., employee equity repurchases capped at $2.5M/year plus key-man insurance proceeds).
plain_english_summary: Cash dividends are effectively blocked; only small employee-equity buybacks are allowed.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No cash prepayment/redemption of 2026 Convertible Notes or Permitted Convertible Indebtedness until Obligations paid in full, except with equity/convertible proceeds or certain Permitted Transfer proceeds; scheduled interest and fractional-share cash carved out. Certain triggers redacted.
plain_english_summary: Coherus generally can't use cash to retire the convertibles ahead of the term loan, with carve-outs for equity/asset-sale proceeds.
notes: Redemption triggers partly redacted [***]; the 2026 Notes were later repaid mainly with UDENYCA sale proceeds (permitted route).

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Affiliate dealings restricted to arm's-length / ordinary-course terms under the negative-covenant framework and Permitted Investments limits.
plain_english_summary: Related-party deals must be arm's-length.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: 10 days' prior notice to change jurisdiction/structure/name; may not move books or Collateral > $1,000,000 FMV to new locations without 15 days' notice and a Collateral Access Agreement.
plain_english_summary: Coherus can't quietly relocate or restructure; moving material collateral needs notice and access agreements.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No merger, consolidation, liquidation or dissolution except intercompany (a Credit Party survives) and Permitted Acquisitions.
plain_english_summary: The company can't merge away or dissolve outside permitted intercompany or acquisition structures.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Transfers of Specified-Product Company IP restricted to Permitted Transfers; no waiver/amendment/termination/breach of Current Company IP Agreements re a Specified Product that could materially adversely impact the product or Credit Party rights (Sec. 6.14).
plain_english_summary: Key product IP can't be transferred or its licenses gutted without tripping the covenant - central protection for a biotech collateral base.
notes: Product-IP licensing baskets (Permitted Transfers r/s/t) partly redacted - the main IP-leakage vector.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No amendment/waiver of Operating Documents reasonably expected to cause a Material Adverse Change.
plain_english_summary: Charter/bylaw changes that could hurt lenders are prohibited.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No knowing action reasonably expected to result in development/commercialization of a LOQTORZI Competitor or UDENYCA Competitor.
plain_english_summary: Coherus can't help create competitors to its own key products.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
threshold_or_amount: Greater of $350,000,000 or 25.0% of Market Capitalization
legal_summary: Convertible-debt basket = greater of $350,000,000 or 25.0% of Market Capitalization (includes the 2026 Convertible Notes). Grower = 25% of market cap.
plain_english_summary: Coherus may carry up to the greater of $350M or 25% of its market value in convertible notes.
notes: Large relative to the ~$38.7M term loan; structurally senior term loan mitigates.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Capex/capital-lease debt basket up to $5,000,000.
plain_english_summary: Up to $5M of equipment/lease financing allowed.
notes: Availability not calculable from public information (no basket usage disclosed).

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Acquisition-financing (subordinated) basket up to $5,000,000; Subordinated Debt basket up to $5,000,000.
plain_english_summary: Two $5M buckets for acquisition and subordinated debt.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Contingent Obligations up to $2.5M / $5.0M; other unsecured debt up to $2,500,000.
plain_english_summary: Small buckets for miscellaneous unsecured and contingent obligations.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: General investment basket up to $2,500,000; Credit-Party-to-non-Credit-Party subsidiary investments up to $2,500,000.
plain_english_summary: Only ~$2.5M of general investments and downstreaming to non-guarantor subs is allowed.
notes: Tight investment baskets limit value leakage to non-guarantors.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Employee equity repurchases up to $2,500,000 per calendar year plus key-man insurance proceeds; no general dividend basket.
plain_english_summary: The only real restricted-payment room is ~$2.5M/year of employee stock buybacks; no dividends.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: 'Other dispositions' basket up to $1,500,000 aggregate fair market value; additional monetization baskets (r),(s),(t) redacted.
plain_english_summary: Only $1.5M of miscellaneous asset sales are freely permitted; larger product monetizations run through redacted baskets.
notes: Redacted baskets (r/s/t) are the material asset/IP-monetization channels - not quantifiable from public info.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: On any Change in Control, prepay in full (principal + accrued interest + Makewhole (if pre-1st anniversary) + Prepayment Premium). Non-consensual CoC: within 2 business days; consensual: contemporaneous with 10 business days' prior notice.
plain_english_summary: A change of control forces full repayment with premium - the only mandatory prepay trigger.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: 'any proceeds from Permitted Transfers shall not require any prepayment of the Term Loan.' No asset-sale sweep.
plain_english_summary: Asset-sale proceeds do NOT have to pay down the loan - a significant leakage risk given Coherus's asset-monetization strategy.
notes: Confirmed in practice: ~$483M UDENYCA sale proceeds did NOT prepay the term loan.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No mandatory prepayment from insurance or condemnation proceeds.
plain_english_summary: No insurance/casualty proceeds sweep.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No mandatory prepayment from debt or equity issuance proceeds.
plain_english_summary: New debt or equity raises don't have to pay down the loan.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No excess-cash-flow sweep.
plain_english_summary: No ECF sweep.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No mandatory prepayment from licensing or royalty-monetization proceeds; no reinvestment framework (moot given no sweeps).
plain_english_summary: Product licensing/royalty monetizations do not have to repay the loan.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
cure_period: 3 business days (interest only); none for principal
legal_summary: Principal non-payment - no cure; interest/premium/other Obligations - 3 business day grace (no cure if due at maturity or on acceleration).
plain_english_summary: Missing principal is a breach with no identified cure period; interest has a 3-business-day grace.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Breach of Sec. 5.2-5.7, 5.10, 5.12-5.14, 5.16-5.17 or any Sec. 6 covenant = no identified cure period; any other covenant = 20-day cure after knowledge or notice.
plain_english_summary: Breaching the core covenants (including minimum liquidity and the negative covenants) is a breach with no identified cure period; other covenants get a 20-day cure.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: A Material Adverse Change is a standalone Event of Default.
plain_english_summary: A material adverse change alone can trigger default - a strong, subjective lender lever.
notes: MAC as a direct EOD (not just a rep) is notably lender-protective.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Trustee process attaching funds > $5,000,000, or lien/levy on material Collateral, not discharged/stayed within 30 days.
plain_english_summary: Large attachments/levies not cleared within 30 days default the loan.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Involuntary proceeding undismissed 60 days; any voluntary bankruptcy/assignment/inability to pay = no cure. Sec. 7.5 triggers automatic acceleration.
plain_english_summary: A voluntary bankruptcy filing automatically accelerates the whole loan (the agreement expressly makes acceleration automatic here).

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Failure to pay other Indebtedness (after its grace) or acceleration by holders where the amount exceeds $5,000,000.
plain_english_summary: A payment failure or acceleration on other debt above $5M cross-defaults this loan.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Final non-appealable money judgment(s) > $5,000,000 (excl. insured/indemnified) not discharged/stayed/bonded within 30 days.
plain_english_summary: Large unpaid court judgments (>$5M) default the loan.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Any representation/warranty incorrect in a material respect (any respect if MAC-qualified).
plain_english_summary: A materially false rep is a breach with no identified cure period.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Any material Loan Document ceasing to be valid/enforceable, or a Collateral Document ceasing to create/maintain a first-priority perfected security interest in material Collateral.
plain_english_summary: If the guarantees or first-priority liens fail, that itself defaults the loan.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: ERISA Event resulting or reasonably expected to result in a Material Adverse Change or a Sec. 303(k) Lien on Collateral.
plain_english_summary: Serious pension/ERISA problems can default the loan.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: On EoD, Collateral Agent may (or at Required Lenders' direction shall), without notice, declare all Obligations due (automatic on insolvency), stop credit, set off and sweep Collateral Accounts (cash dominion), take/sell Collateral with an IP license, and exercise all Code remedies; Makewhole + Prepayment Premium due on acceleration.
plain_english_summary: On default the lenders can accelerate, seize cash in controlled accounts, and foreclose - including an operating license over the IP.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No Event of Default on the 2029 Term Loan at 9/30/2025, 12/31/2025 or 3/31/2026; no amendments or waivers to the 2029 Loan Agreement disclosed.
plain_english_summary: The loan is performing with no defaults or waivers to date.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
agreement_name: Revenue Participation Right Purchase and Sale Agreement
legal_summary: Sale of the right to receive the Revenue Percentage of U.S. Net Sales of UDENYCA (pegfilgrastim-cbqv) and LOQTORZI (toripalimab-tpzi); Coduet Royalty Holdings, LLC as Buyer Representative (Barings-managed); Ankura as Paying Agent (added by Omnibus Amendment).
plain_english_summary: A SEPARATE royalty monetization: Coherus sold a slice of U.S. UDENYCA and LOQTORZI net sales to a Barings-managed buyer.
notes: Distinct agreement from the Loan Agreement; junior to it via the Intercreditor Agreement.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
agreement_name: Revenue Participation Right Purchase and Sale Agreement
legal_summary: Purchase Price = $37,500,000 (per-product UDENYCA/LOQTORZI allocation redacted).
plain_english_summary: Coherus received $37.5M upfront for the royalty.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
agreement_name: Revenue Participation Right Purchase and Sale Agreement
legal_summary: Tiered per product per year: 5.0% on first [***] of Net Sales, 0.5% above; UDENYCA rises to 6.50% on a 'Udenyca Step-Up Event' (loss of OnBody/Sorrel supply). Tier dollar thresholds redacted.
plain_english_summary: The buyer gets 5% of U.S. net sales (0.5% above a sales cap), with a UDENYCA step-up to 6.5% if the on-body injector supply is lost.
notes: Tier thresholds redacted [***].

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
agreement_name: Revenue Participation Right Purchase and Sale Agreement
legal_summary: Investment Return Amount = 2.25 x the original Purchase Price allocated to each Product; the obligation for a product ends once the buyer collects 2.25x.
plain_english_summary: Total payout is capped at 2.25x the purchase price per product.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
agreement_name: Revenue Participation Right Purchase and Sale Agreement
legal_summary: Runs product-by-product from the Effective Date until the buyer receives the Investment Return Amount, then auto-terminates and Collateral reverts; no fixed calendar end.
plain_english_summary: It ends when the 2.25x cap is reached for each product - no fixed maturity date.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
agreement_name: Revenue Participation Right Purchase and Sale Agreement
legal_summary: Structured as a true sale with a back-up security interest in the Revenue Participation Right and Revenue Payments; Omnibus Amendment added a Paying Agent security interest over the Collection Account; lien/remedies subject to the Intercreditor Agreement (junior to the term loan).
plain_english_summary: The royalty is a 'true sale' but backed by liens that sit behind the term lenders.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
agreement_name: Revenue Participation Right Purchase and Sale Agreement
legal_summary: On CoC to an Acceptable Assignee, pay Purchase Price less payments made; to a non-Acceptable Assignee, pay the full Investment Return Amount (2.25x) less payments. On a Material Adverse Effect or breach of specified reps, buyer may accelerate and demand the 2.25x amount.
plain_english_summary: A change of control or material adverse event can accelerate the royalty to the full 2.25x cap.
notes: Change-of-control threshold = 35% beneficial ownership (partly redacted proviso).

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
agreement_name: Revenue Participation Right Purchase and Sale Agreement
legal_summary: Amends the RPRPSA and the Security and Paying Agent Agreement: adds Ankura Trust Company as Paying Agent, creates a Paying Agent Security Interest over a segregated Collection Account, defines 'Payment Date' (15th day of the 3rd month after quarter-end), and sets monthly-sweep mechanics; permanent, not a novation.
plain_english_summary: The June 2024 amendment formalized a paying-agent/lockbox structure for the royalty payments - permanent, with no economic loosening.
notes: Amends the Revenue Participation documents, NOT the term Loan Agreement.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
agreement_name: Revenue Participation Right Purchase and Sale Agreement
legal_summary: UDENYCA portion of the revenue-participation right terminated/bought out April 15, 2025 for a $47.7M payment funded from UDENYCA sale proceeds ($17,283K derecognized). LOQTORZI portion remains active (net carrying ~$14.4M at 3/31/2026).
plain_english_summary: Coherus paid $47.7M in April 2025 to buy back the UDENYCA royalty; only the LOQTORZI royalty (~$14.4M) remains.
notes: Modification/partial termination of the Revenue Participation Agreement, not the Loan Agreement.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: UDENYCA sold to Intas Pharmaceuticals Ltd. (assets to Accord BioPharma); agreement Dec 2, 2024, closed April 11, 2025; $483.4M upfront (incl. $118.4M inventory) plus two $37.5M net-sales earnouts. Proceeds retired the 2026 Convertible Notes and funded the UDENYCA royalty buy-out - NOT the term loan.
plain_english_summary: Case study in the no-sweep design: Coherus sold UDENYCA (a key collateral product) for ~$483M plus earnouts and redeployed the cash on the convertibles and royalty buy-out rather than repaying the term loan.
notes: Reclassified as an asset-sale case study (not a Loan Agreement amendment). Demonstrates asset-sale leakage risk; LOQTORZI is now the sole commercial product.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: LOQTORZI (toripalimab-tpzi), FDA-approved PD-1 inhibitor, is Coherus's sole commercial product post-UDENYCA sale; U.S./Canada rights via Junshi; net revenue $40.8M FY2025, $11.8M Q1 2026 (growing).
plain_english_summary: The credit now rests on a single product, LOQTORZI, still small but growing.
notes: Single-product concentration is the key forward credit risk.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Cash and equivalents $115.2M and total cash/equivalents/marketable securities $167.0M at 3/31/2026. 2026 Convertible Notes reduced from $230M to ~$0.1M (repurchased $170M + $59.9M in 2025); remaining $0.1M matured April 15, 2026.
plain_english_summary: Coherus holds ~$167M of liquidity and has effectively retired its convertible notes, leaving the ~$38.7M term loan as the main funded debt.
notes: Reported cash is not identical to covenant 'Liquidity' (unrestricted cash in Collateral Accounts).

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No going-concern qualification or 'substantial doubt' language; management states liquidity is sufficient for at least 12 months from the issuance date.
plain_english_summary: Auditors and management report no going-concern doubt.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
applicable_entity: Barings BDC, Inc. (BBDC) and Barings-managed funds (lenders)
legal_summary: Barings confirmed as lender (per Latham & Watkins deal announcement and Barings funds' Schedules of Investments). Barings BDC (CIK 1379785) lists Coherus in 8+ filings (10-Qs through 2026-05-07, 10-Ks 2025/2026). Sister funds show the tranche: 'Coherus - Term Loan', SOFR + 8.00%, maturing 5/8/2029 (e.g., Barings Corporate Investors par $598,648, FV $594,457 at 12/31/2025; Barings Participation Investors par $299,324 at 9/30/2025), plus Coduet Royalty Holdings SPV equity.
plain_english_summary: Barings BDC is a confirmed lender in the Coherus term loan syndicate at SOFR+8% maturing May 2029; its exact dollar slice isn't line-item extractable from the public tooling but sister Barings funds confirm the terms.
notes: BBDC's specific par/cost/fair value not extractable via available tooling (SOI tables truncate); confirmed exposure and terms via co-investing Barings vehicles MCI/MPV.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: First-priority all-asset lien (incl. IP), guarantees from the four named Credit Parties, cash-dominion via account control, MAC event of default, no-cure covenant defaults on core covenants, and a small ($38.7M) loan against ~$167M liquidity.
plain_english_summary: Structurally the lenders are well protected: senior secured, guaranteed, with cash control and fast-acting, low-cure defaults on a small, well-covered loan.
notes: Balanced-to-lender-friendly on structure; the gap is the covenant/prepayment package, not the security.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Sole financial maintenance covenant is $15M minimum liquidity; no leverage, coverage, revenue or EBITDA test. Coverage against the floor appears ample on reported cash but is indicative, since the covenant counts only unrestricted cash in Collateral Accounts.
plain_english_summary: The financial covenant package is thin - one liquidity floor and nothing tied to performance - so it gives little early warning; and even the headroom is indicative because only unrestricted cash in controlled accounts counts.
notes: Largest single weakness in the covenant architecture.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Blanket first-priority lien on substantially all assets including product IP (USPTO-recorded), equity pledges, and account control; senior to the Coduet royalty via intercreditor.
plain_english_summary: The collateral package is strong and senior, though its value now concentrates in LOQTORZI after UDENYCA was sold.
notes: Security-agreement enumerated collateral/excluded property redacted in public filing.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: The four named guarantors - Coherus Intermediate Corp., Intekrin Therapeutics Inc., Surface Oncology, LLC, and Coherus Oncology Supportive Care LLC - guarantee and pledge; the covenant sweeps in future subs (other than <$2M-asset Excluded Subs and named Coherus Immunology LLC) within 45 days. Surface Oncology is the sole significant sub per Ex-21.1.
plain_english_summary: Guarantee coverage runs to the four named subsidiary guarantors and any future material subs across a small subsidiary group.
notes: Simple corporate structure limits structural-subordination risk.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No asset-sale, ECF, insurance, debt/equity, or licensing-proceeds prepayment sweep; only change-of-control forces repayment. Demonstrated by ~$483M UDENYCA proceeds bypassing the term loan.
plain_english_summary: Value can leak: Coherus sold its biggest product and kept/redeployed the cash without repaying the loan - the clearest documentary hole.
notes: This is the provision most likely to matter alongside single-product concentration.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Convertible basket = greater of $350M or 25% of market cap (structurally junior/unsecured); secured incremental capacity is limited to small ($5M-class) capex/acquisition/subordinated baskets.
plain_english_summary: Coherus can raise sizable convertible (junior) debt but very little additional secured debt ahead of the lenders.
notes: Large convertible basket is mitigated by the term loan's senior secured position.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: No dividend basket; only ~$2.5M/year employee equity repurchases (plus key-man insurance). Junior/convertible cash redemptions blocked until the loan is repaid, subject to equity/asset-sale-proceeds carve-outs.
plain_english_summary: Cash can't be dividended out, and convertibles generally can't be cash-redeemed ahead of the loan - though asset-sale proceeds are a carve-out that was used.
notes: The equity/asset-proceeds carve-out to the junior-redemption block is where cash actually exited.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Dispositions limited to Permitted Transfers, but several material monetization/licensing baskets ((r),(s),(t)) are redacted and carry no prepayment sweep; general basket only $1.5M.
plain_english_summary: On paper transfers are restricted, but redacted monetization baskets plus the absence of a sweep let Coherus monetize major assets without paying down debt.
notes: Redacted baskets prevent full quantification; treat as a material leakage channel.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Product IP is collateral and Sec. 6.14 restricts gutting IP agreements, but the redacted Permitted Transfer baskets and the licensing/out-license framework allow IP monetization; no IP-proceeds sweep.
plain_english_summary: The key risk vector for a biotech: product IP could be licensed/monetized through permitted baskets without repaying lenders.
notes: Most likely path for future value transfer given Coherus's monetization playbook.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Quarterly (45-day) and annual (90-day) financials plus compliance certificates and prompt default/litigation/IP notices, but no monthly reporting, no budget/projection delivery, and going-concern qualifications expressly permitted.
plain_english_summary: Reporting is adequate but public-company-standard - no budgets, no monthly numbers, and a permitted going-concern audit qualification.
notes: Forward-looking visibility (budgets/projections) is absent.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: MAC standalone EoD, no-cure covenant defaults on core covenants, $5M cross-default/judgment thresholds, automatic acceleration on insolvency, cash dominion on default.
plain_english_summary: Default triggers are sensitive and lender-favorable - a MAC or covenant slip is a breach with no identified cure period, backed by fast enforcement.
notes: Offsets some of the thin financial-covenant package by giving lenders early, discretionary exit levers.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Standard Required-Lenders amendment regime with sacred-rights protections; concentrated Barings-controlled syndicate. Only amendment to date is the June 2024 royalty Omnibus (no term-loan amendments/waivers).
plain_english_summary: Amendment risk is average; the concentrated Barings holder group cuts both ways but no covenant relief has been needed.
notes: No term-loan amendments or waivers granted through Q1 2026.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: The combination of a first-priority all-asset/IP lien with cash dominion (account control) and a MAC event of default: lenders can seize controlled cash and accelerate on a discretionary adverse-change judgment.
plain_english_summary: The strongest protection is senior secured cash control plus a MAC default - lenders can act fast and reach the cash.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
legal_summary: Absence of any proceeds-based mandatory prepayment (no asset-sale/ECF/licensing sweep) combined with only a single $15M liquidity covenant - proven when ~$483M of UDENYCA proceeds bypassed the loan.
plain_english_summary: The biggest hole is that Coherus can sell core assets and keep the cash, with no performance covenant to catch deterioration.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
provision_name: Provision most likely to become relevant first
legal_summary: The no-sweep asset-transfer/IP-monetization framework (Sec. 2.2(c)(i) and redacted Permitted Transfers) against a now single-product (LOQTORZI) collateral base; secondarily the $15M minimum liquidity floor.
plain_english_summary: What bites first is likely another asset/IP monetization draining value, watched against the $15M liquidity floor - not a performance covenant, because there isn't one.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
plain_english_summary: This CSV analyzes the 2024 Loan Agreement (senior secured term loan due 2029) as the controlling credit agreement, and separately captures the related Revenue Participation Right Purchase and Sale Agreement (rows carry the agreement_name 'Revenue Participation Right Purchase and Sale Agreement') plus its June 2024 Omnibus Amendment. Loan Agreement rows and Revenue Participation rows are distinguished by the agreement_name column so the two financings are not conflated.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
provision_name: Summary - What lenders are protected by
plain_english_summary: Lenders hold a senior, first-priority security interest in substantially all assets - including product intellectual property - backed by guarantees from the four named subsidiary guarantors (Coherus Intermediate Corp., Intekrin Therapeutics Inc., Surface Oncology, LLC, and Coherus Oncology Supportive Care LLC) and by deposit-account control that lets them sweep cash on default. The loan is small (~$38.7M) relative to Coherus's ~$167M of reported liquidity, and default triggers are aggressive: a standalone material-adverse-change event of default, no-cure defaults on the core covenants, $5M cross-default and judgment thresholds, automatic acceleration on insolvency, and a make-whole/prepayment premium payable on acceleration. Priority over the Coduet revenue-participation buyer is locked in by an intercreditor agreement.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
provision_name: Summary - What management can still do
plain_english_summary: A great deal. There is no leverage, coverage, revenue, or EBITDA covenant - only a $15M minimum-liquidity floor, which reported cash clears comfortably (indicatively, since the covenant counts only unrestricted cash in controlled Collateral Accounts). Critically, no class of proceeds (asset sales, licensing/royalty monetizations, insurance, debt or equity issuance, or excess cash flow) must be used to prepay the loan; only a change of control forces repayment. Management can therefore monetize products and IP through permitted-transfer baskets (several of which are redacted) and redeploy the cash, can raise a large amount of convertible/junior debt (the greater of $350M or 25% of market cap), and can retire junior debt with equity or asset-sale proceeds - as it in fact did.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
provision_name: Summary - Where value could leak outside the collateral package
plain_english_summary: The clearest leakage path is asset and IP monetization without a repayment sweep. Coherus proved this in 2025: it sold UDENYCA - a key collateral product - to Intas/Accord for ~$483M upfront plus earnouts and used the proceeds to retire the 2026 convertible notes and buy out the UDENYCA royalty ($47.7M), leaving the term loan fully outstanding. The redacted Permitted Transfer baskets ((r),(s),(t)) and the licensing framework are the mechanisms; the missing mandatory-prepayment package is what makes leakage possible. Value now concentrates in a single remaining product, LOQTORZI.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
provision_name: Summary - Which covenant or trigger matters first
plain_english_summary: Because there is no performance covenant, the first live risk is another asset/IP monetization draining collateral value rather than a covenant breach. The $15M minimum-liquidity floor is the only financial tripwire and, on reported cash, is not close to being hit (though the qualifying amount is narrower than total cash). If credit deteriorates, lenders are more likely to rely on the discretionary material-adverse-change event of default and cash-dominion rights than on any numeric covenant - so the MAC clause, not a ratio, is the practical early lever.

company: Coherus Oncology, Inc. (f/k/a Coherus BioSciences, Inc.)
plain_english_summary: On structure the agreement is lender-friendly - senior secured, guaranteed, cash-dominion, MAC default, small balance, strong liquidity. On the covenant and prepayment package it is decidedly borrower-friendly - a single liquidity covenant, no proceeds sweeps, generous junior-debt capacity, and permitted asset/IP monetization. Netting these, the facility is best characterized as BALANCED but structure-heavy: the lenders are well secured and can exit quickly, but they rely on collateral value and discretionary triggers rather than on covenants to police the borrower, and Coherus retains wide latitude to move assets and cash so long as it stays solvent and above $15M of qualifying liquidity. This is not legal advice.
